product_overview: Hairconnekt is a two-sided mobile marketplace (clients and providers) that connects clients with independent hair service providers (stylists, barbers, braiders) to discover, book, and manage appointments while giving providers tools to manage services, schedules, and earnings. (See executive summary and features listed across pages 1-3 of the source PRD.)
core_goals: Provide a seamless discovery and booking experience for clients, including search, filters, and real-time availability (see Client App Features on page 1-2).
Empower providers to manage services, availability, appointments, and finances through an intuitive provider dashboard (see Provider App Features on page 2).
Increase provider monetization and platform revenue via subscription tiers and commission model described on page 2.
Ship a robust, testable mobile application using the existing TypeScript/React Native/Expo stack with clean separation between domain, data, and presentation layers (see code summary).
Ensure secure authentication, payments, and reliable API integration (JWT auth, Stripe implied, see pages 2-3 and codebase APIs).
key_features: Authentication & Onboarding: Email/phone registration, JWT-based authentication, potential social login (source PRD pages 1-2 and src/auth).
Client Discovery & Search: Map view, search with filters (style, location, availability), style galleries and provider profiles (MapViewScreen and client APIs in codebase).
Client Booking Flow: Service selection, calendar availability, booking creation, payment integration (clientBooking API and screens).
Provider Services Management: CRUD for services, pricing and duration validation, ServicesManagementScreen and related usecases/repository in codebase.
Provider Appointments & Calendar: Appointment management, block time, providerAppointments API and provider screens.
Profiles, Portfolios & Reviews: Provider portfolios, image uploads (Cloudflare R2), write/view reviews (WriteReviewsScreen referenced on page 2).
Provider Dashboard & Analytics: Booking and revenue analytics, earnings/payout history, vouchers and subscription management (AnalyticsDashboardScreen, ProviderSubscriptionScreen).
Shared Design System & Reusable Components: Buttons, Inputs, Avatar, Typography components for consistent UI (src/components files).
API Integration & HTTP Client: Centralized http adapter, config, and API endpoints (src/api/http.ts, adapters, config).
Monetization: Subscription tiers (Basic/Pro/Premium) and commission fees described on page 2.
user_flow_summary: Client onboarding: register/login → complete profile → browse styles or search → view provider profile → select service → view availability → book appointment → pay (clientBooking API). (See pages 1-2.)

Provider onboarding: register/login → set up profile and services → configure availability/calendar and block time → receive/manage bookings → fulfill appointments → view earnings and analytics (ServicesManagementScreen, providerAppointments API).
Service management flow (provider): create/edit/delete service → validate price/duration → publish to client discovery (ServiceUseCases and ServiceRepositoryImpl).

Appointment flow (both sides): create appointment → provider confirms or manages schedule → reminders/notifications (appointments screens and provider API) → mark complete and optionally write review.

Payment & monetization flow: client selects payment method → process via integrated payment gateway (Stripe implied) → platform applies commission and tracks provider payout and subscription billing (ProviderSubscriptionScreen and payments references on page 2-3).
validation_criteria: Authentication: Users can register, login, and maintain session using JWT; unit tests for auth flows (coverage via Jest).
Service Management: Providers can create/update/delete services; validations enforce price > 0, duration within allowed bounds; integration tests with ServiceRepositoryImpl and usecases pass.
Booking & Availability: Clients can only book available slots; booking creation persists and notifies provider; end-to-end test covering clientBooking and providerAppointments endpoints.
Payments: Payment flow completes successfully in sandbox (Stripe) and transactions reflect commissions/subscription charges.
Provider Dashboard: Analytics metrics (weekly bookings, gross revenue) surface accurate values derived from stored appointments and earnings.
UI/UX: Core screens render and function across supported devices (unit and snapshot tests for shared components Button/Input/Avatar).
API Stability: HTTP client and API adapters handle errors and retries; integration tests for critical endpoints defined in src/api.
Security & Privacy: JWT tokens securely issued and validated; image uploads stored in Cloudflare R2 (or equivalent) with access controls.
Performance: Key flows (search, booking creation) respond within acceptable SLAs for mobile (e.g., <2s for search results under normal conditions).
Release Readiness: Documented feature toggles, migration scripts (if any), and CI test pass threshold >= 90% before production deploy.

features.name: Provider Services Management
features.description: Manage services offered by providers, including CRUD operations and validation
